# National Flood Response Policy Extract

## Authority and approval

Only designated government authorities can issue official evacuation orders, public warnings, or road closure mandates. Decision-support systems may recommend actions for review, but they must not present recommendations as official instructions.

## Conflicting evidence

When a local field report conflicts with a policy document, planners should treat the policy as the governing authority and use field reports as supporting context. Conflicts must be surfaced in the handover brief.

## Data confidence

Live weather feeds, field observations, and model predictions each have different uncertainty. Handover reports should name the evidence source and avoid claiming verified disaster status unless confirmed by the responsible authority.
